@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly retrieving 2015 metadata and article texts and merging them, the agent used a simplistic keyword-based classifier in execute_python that defaults to 'World' when no keywords match, causing misclassification and incorrect regional counts, resulting in "Europe" instead of the correct "Africa."
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent correctly retrieved 2015 metadata and article texts but in execute_python used regex patterns with r"\\b" around keywords, causing no keyword matches and defaulting most articles to "World," which skewed counts and led to the wrong top region.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the execute_python step, the agent merged 2015 metadata with only five article texts from the articles collection and used a keyword classifier that defaults unmatched or missing text to "World," causing widespread misclassification and an incorrect top region ("Europe") instead of the ground truth ("Africa").
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent computed the 2015 top region using a naive keyword-based classifier in execute_python that defaults to "World" when no sports/business/tech keywords match (and even mislabels many world stories as "Business" via keywords like "reuters -"), leading to misclassification and an incorrect top region.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly pulling 2015 article metadata, the agent fetched only five unfiltered articles from the articles collection (call_pm8bUbFTfliwACrNwuneM9uD), so the subsequent classification/counting (call_h0mkhZCQEJvQh58LrN1vc7ja) had no matching texts for the 2015 IDs and returned "No World articles found in 2015" instead of counting regions (where Africa is largest).
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no

FM3: no
FM4: yes
